items.title: War in Iran Approaches 100 Day Mark
items.summary: American forces headed off Iranian missile and drone attacks in the Persian Gulf as the war that began nearly 100 days ago continued to simmer with no resolution in the offing. Six ballistic missiles fired at Bahrain and Kuwait “were intercepted and a seventh did not reach its intended target,” US Central Command said in a statement on X late Friday. In D-Day speech, Hegseth urges Europe to counter ‘invasion’ of migrants
items.summary:
US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth on Saturday urged Europe to counter what he termed an “invasion” of its coastline by migration, as he marked the 82nd anniversary of the World War II D-Day landings in northern France.
Hegseth also called on European countries to do more to contribute to their defence, in a speech at the American military cemetery in Colleville-sur-mer in Normandy.
He was however

items.title: How Bolivia's Plans to Tap Mineral Riches Are Being Tested by Mass Unrest
items.summary: Bolivia’s new president, Rodrigo Paz, is attempting to rebrand the country as a destination for foreign investment after two decades of socialist rule. Sitting atop some of the world’s largest lithium resources and vast mineral reserves, Bolivia has long struggled to translate its natural resources into economic prosperity. Trump Holds Profanity-Filled Call with Netanyahu
items.summary: President Donald Trump has spent months projecting confidence that a deal to end the Iran conflict is within reach. Israel’s determination to press ahead with its own objectives underscores his tenuous control over the outcome of the crisis. Former US ambassador to Israel under Obama, Tom Nides, joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Pope Leo urges end to ‘polarising narratives’ on Spain visit
items.summary:
Pope Leo on Saturday urged global leaders to avoid dividing their electorates with “sterile simplifications” to gain popularity and called on them to listen to the world’s cries for peace, in a forceful speech opening a week-long tour of Spain.

items.title:
RT by @MFA_China: Chinese Premier Li Qiang met with General Secretary of the Lao People’s Revolutionary Party Central Committee and Lao President Thongloun Sisoulith in Beijing.

China is willing to strengthen the alignment of development strategies with Laos, fully unleash the positive effects of China’s zero-tariff policy, continue to expand bilateral trade, further advance cooperation on the China-Laos Railway, accelerate the development of the China-Laos Economic Corridor, and expand cooperation in energy and mineral resources, artificial intelligence, the digital economy and other fields to deliver more tangible results.

🔗https://www.fmprc.gov.cn/zyxw/202606/t20260605_11938598.shtml
